mnemonics: WOODS & EDDINS: Woods you EDDit an image? (You process it with digital techniques)
keyPoints: Authors of the foundational textbook 'Digital Image Processing'
Their work covers core algorithms for image enhancement, restoration, and compression
They established standard practices for representing and manipulating digital images in the spatial and frequency domains
commonMistakes: Confusing them with the original authors of the field (like Gonzalez) - Woods & Eddins are also major textbook authors
Assuming their work is only about high-level vision concepts; it's focused on the fundamental, low-level processing techniques
Overlooking the importance of their mathematical framework for operations like convolution and Fourier analysis
